lede: A look at July 25 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Rosalind Franklin and Toni Duggan, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: WikiLeaks
events.desc: WikiLeaks publishes classified documents about the War in Afghanistan, one of the largest leaks in U.S. military history.

events.title: Pratibha Patil
events.desc: Pratibha Patil is sworn in as India's first female president.

events.title: Phoolan Devi
events.desc: Phoolan Devi, a serving Member of Parliament, was assassinated by shooting in New Delhi, India.

events.title: Bob Dylan
events.desc: Bob Dylan goes electric at the Newport Folk Festival, signaling a major change in folk and rock music.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Benito Mussolini is forced out of office by the King (encouraged by the Grand Council of Fascism) and is replaced by Pietro Badoglio.

events.title: Spanish–American War
events.desc: Spanish–American War: The American invasion of Spanish-held Puerto Rico begins, as United States Army troops under General Nelson A. Miles land and secure the port at Guánica.

events.title: Napoleon
events.desc: Napoleon Bonaparte defeats a numerically superior Ottoman army under Mustafa Pasha at the Battle of Abukir.

events.title: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
events.desc: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart completes his Symphony No. 40 in G minor (K550).

events.title: Henry II of France
events.desc: Henry II of France is crowned.

events.title: Constantine the Great
events.desc: Constantine I is proclaimed Roman emperor by his troops.

births.name: Rosalind Franklin
births.line: English biophysicist, chemist, and academic. Contributor to basic research of DNA.

births.name: Arthur Balfour
births.line: Scottish-English lieutenant and politician, 33rd Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

births.name: Ibn Arabi
births.line: Andalusian Sufi mystic, poet, and philosopher

deaths.text: Randy Pausch, American computer scientist and educator
Vladimir Vysotsky, Russian singer-songwriter, guitarist, and actor
Engelbert Dollfuss, Austrian politician, 14th Chancellor of Austria

observances.text: Christian feast day: James the Great (Western Christianity)
Christian feast day: Julian of Le Mans (translation)
